_contract: The registered inventory of repo-internal vocabulary that dimension 3 gates
(SPEC-article-draft-pipeline CAP-7; quality-rubric.md dimension 3; #305).
This file IS the contract, not a convenience cache. Dimension 3 is only as
exhaustive as this inventory, so registration is mandatory: introducing a new
internal stage name, framework ID, marker, diagram label, or pipeline term
means adding it here in the same change. check-internal-vocabulary.sh derives
the identifier families that CAN be derived from canonical code structures
(framework IDs, pipeline stage names, markers) and FAILS when one of them is
not covered here -- so unknown internal vocabulary cannot silently bypass the
gate by drifting away from the code that introduced it.
Prose nouns (fact sheet, editorial anchor, ...) have no canonical machine
source to derive from; for those this file is the source of truth and the
drift check enforces its shape, not its completeness. That boundary is stated
rather than papered over: see the check's closing report.
Ordinary-English collisions are deliberately NOT registered as bare words.
'derived', 'verify', 'fill' and 'interview' are provenance classes and stage
names internally, but gating those bare words would manufacture false blockers
in ordinary prose -- worse than the gap they would close. Their unambiguous
compound forms are registered instead ('provenance map', 'sourced claim',
'framework fill', 'gap interview'), and the drift check accepts a derived
identifier as covered when a registered compound names it. So the stage is
always accounted for; the inventory only chooses the surface that is safe to
gate.

derived_families._note: Asserted by check-internal-vocabulary.sh against canonical code structures.
derived_families.markers: [VERIFY] and NEEDS-OWNER, the two owner-facing draft markers
